title: Cenovus, Enbridge Accused of 'Greenwashing' Under Securities Law in What May be a First for Canada
content: A complaint filed with Albertaâ€™s securities regulator says Cenovus Energy Inc. and Enbridge Inc. misled investors by exaggerating or misrepresenting their net-zero commitments. Investors for Paris Compliance (I4PC), a climate-focused shareholder group, filed the complaint on Tuesday in what is believed to be the first attempt in Canada to use securities law to penalize energy companies for â€œgreenwashing.â€ Cenovus and Enbridgeâ€™s pledges to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2050 arenâ€™t credible, the group said in its complaint, since the companies omit emissions from customers burning fuels (called â€œscope 3 emissionsâ€) in their targets and offer scant detail about concrete plans to achieve their ambitions.
author: Aru Somya
